words.simpleMeaning: to be real or present

sentences.daily: Does a perfect job really exist?
sentences.office: The old file no longer exists in the system.
sentences.interview: I believe that a solution exists for every problem.
sentences.business: Opportunities exist in every market.
sentences.formal: Such a provision does not exist in the contract.

words.usageNote: Use 'exist' to say something is real/present; pair with 'there' structures.

words.simpleMeaning: to find the position of something

sentences.daily: I couldn't locate the keys.
sentences.office: The IT team located the error quickly.
sentences.interview: Can you locate the relevant files?
sentences.business: We located a new vendor.
sentences.formal: The authority located the source of the leak.

words.usageNote: 'Locate' is more formal than 'find'.

words.simpleMeaning: ready to use or obtain

sentences.daily: Is this seat available?
sentences.office: The manager is not available right now.
sentences.interview: Are you available for an immediate joining?
sentences.business: Stock is available in all sizes.
sentences.formal: The report will be available by Friday.

words.usageNote: 'Available' is an adjective — use after 'be' verb.

words.simpleMeaning: not enough of something

sentences.daily: There is a shortage of water in summer.
sentences.office: There is a shortage of staff this month.
sentences.interview: Have you handled a shortage of resources?
sentences.business: There is a shortage of raw material.
sentences.formal: The shortage of skilled workers is concerning.
sentences.informal: There's a total shortage of coffee here!

words.usageNote: 'Shortage' is a noun; 'short of' is the phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: enough for the purpose

sentences.daily: There is sufficient food for everyone.
sentences.office: Is there sufficient budget for this project?
sentences.interview: I have sufficient experience for this role.
sentences.business: There is sufficient demand in the market.

words.usageNote: 'Sufficient' is slightly formal; 'enough' is more common in speech.

words.simpleMeaning: not being in a place

sentences.daily: In his absence, I managed everything.
sentences.office: Her absence affected the team's output.
sentences.interview: Please explain any gap or absence on your CV.
sentences.business: The absence of a clear strategy caused delays.
sentences.formal: The absence of the defendant was noted.

words.usageNote: 'In the absence of' = when something is not there.

words.simpleMeaning: being in a place at a time

sentences.daily: His presence made the party lively.
sentences.office: Your presence is required at the meeting.
sentences.interview: Maintain a confident presence during the interview.
sentences.business: We have a strong market presence.
sentences.formal: The presence of a legal witness is necessary.

words.usageNote: 'Presence of mind' = ability to stay calm.

words.simpleMeaning: an empty space or available job

sentences.daily: Is there a vacancy at the front desk?
sentences.office: There is a vacancy in the IT department.
sentences.interview: I saw the vacancy on your website.
sentences.business: We have a vacancy for a sales manager.
sentences.formal: Vacancy exists for the post of accountant.

words.usageNote: Plural: 'vacancies'. 'There is a vacancy' = job available.

words.simpleMeaning: the maximum amount something can hold

sentences.daily: The hall has a capacity of 200.
sentences.office: There is no capacity for more work.
sentences.interview: What is your capacity to work under pressure?
sentences.business: Our production capacity has doubled.
sentences.formal: The warehouse has a storage capacity of 5000 units.
sentences.informal: I'm at full capacity right now!

words.usageNote: 'At full capacity' = no more space or ability available.

words.simpleMeaning: more than what is needed

sentences.daily: There is a surplus of vegetables today.
sentences.office: We have a budget surplus this quarter.
sentences.interview: How do you manage surplus resources?
sentences.business: There is a surplus of inventory.
sentences.formal: The surplus amount will be carried forward.

words.usageNote: 'Budget surplus' vs 'budget deficit'. Opposite of shortage.

words.simpleMeaning: something that blocks progress

sentences.daily: There are many obstacles on this road.
sentences.office: There is an obstacle in the project approval.
sentences.interview: Tell me about an obstacle you overcame.
sentences.business: There are obstacles in entering this market.
sentences.formal: The obstacle must be removed before proceeding.

words.usageNote: 'Obstacle course' in sports; 'overcome obstacles' in general.

words.simpleMeaning: an answer to a problem

sentences.daily: There is a solution to every problem.
sentences.office: Is there a solution to the network issue?
sentences.interview: I always look for a practical solution.
sentences.business: There is a solution-focused approach.
sentences.formal: A solution must be devised promptly.

words.usageNote: 'Find a solution' or 'come up with a solution'.

words.simpleMeaning: a chance to do something

sentences.daily: There is an opportunity to learn here.
sentences.office: There is a great opportunity in this project.
sentences.interview: I see this role as a great opportunity.
sentences.business: There are many business opportunities abroad.

words.usageNote: Plural: 'opportunities'. 'Miss an opportunity' = lose a chance.

words.simpleMeaning: something difficult that requires effort

sentences.daily: There are many challenges in daily life.
sentences.office: There is a new challenge every day at work.
sentences.interview: I enjoy challenges; they help me grow.
sentences.business: There are challenges in scaling the business.
sentences.formal: The challenge must be addressed systematically.

words.usageNote: 'Challenge' can be a noun or verb. As verb: 'to challenge someone'.

words.simpleMeaning: a supply of something useful

sentences.daily: There are many resources online.
sentences.office: Are there enough resources for the project?
sentences.interview: I can work efficiently with limited resources.
sentences.business: There are abundant natural resources here.
sentences.formal: The resource allocation must be reviewed.

words.usageNote: Plural form 'resources' is most common.

words.simpleMeaning: a building or service for a purpose

sentences.daily: There is a sports facility near my house.
sentences.office: Are there enough facilities in the office?
sentences.interview: What facilities does the company provide?
sentences.business: There is a production facility in Pune.
sentences.formal: The facility must comply with safety norms.

words.usageNote: 'Facilities' (plural) usually refers to services; singular for a specific place.

words.simpleMeaning: a strong request or need for something

sentences.daily: There is a huge demand for good teachers.
sentences.office: There is a demand for more staff.
sentences.interview: I work well even under demanding conditions.
sentences.business: There is a high demand for this product.
sentences.formal: The demand for skilled workers is increasing.

words.usageNote: 'Supply and demand' is a basic economic concept.

words.simpleMeaning: the amount of something available

sentences.daily: There is a fresh supply of vegetables.
sentences.office: There is a supply chain issue.
sentences.interview: How would you handle a disrupted supply?
sentences.business: There is a reliable supply from the vendor.
sentences.formal: The supply must meet the current demand.

words.usageNote: 'Supply chain' is a common business term.

words.simpleMeaning: a feeling of worry or something to worry about

sentences.daily: There is a concern about the weather.
sentences.office: There is a serious concern about the deadline.
sentences.interview: Is there any concern about my qualifications?
sentences.business: There is a concern about market volatility.
sentences.formal: The concern must be addressed immediately.

words.usageNote: 'Concerning' as adjective means 'worrying'.

words.simpleMeaning: something helpful or useful

sentences.daily: There is a great benefit to exercise.
sentences.office: There are many employee benefits here.
sentences.interview: What benefits does this role offer?
sentences.business: There are tax benefits for startups.
sentences.formal: The benefit of this approach is clear.

words.usageNote: 'Benefit of the doubt' = assuming the best.

words.simpleMeaning: the possibility of something bad happening

sentences.daily: There is a risk of rain today.
sentences.office: There is a risk in this investment.
sentences.interview: I analyse risk before making decisions.
sentences.business: There are financial risks in this deal.
sentences.formal: The risk must be mitigated before launch.

words.usageNote: 'At risk' = in danger. 'Risk-free' = without danger.

words.simpleMeaning: being late or taking longer than expected

sentences.daily: There is a delay in the bus today.
sentences.office: There has been a delay in the report.
sentences.interview: How do you handle project delays?
sentences.business: There is a delay in shipment.

words.usageNote: 'Delay' can be a noun and a verb.

words.simpleMeaning: making something better than before

sentences.daily: There is a great improvement in his English.
sentences.office: There is room for improvement in the process.
sentences.interview: I focus on continuous improvement.
sentences.business: There has been an improvement in sales.

words.usageNote: 'Room for improvement' is a common phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: the right or ability to use or enter

sentences.daily: There is no access to this road.
sentences.office: Is there access to the server from home?
sentences.interview: Do I have access to training materials?
sentences.business: There is easy access to the target market.
sentences.formal: Access must be granted by the administrator.

words.usageNote: 'Access to' (not 'access of').

words.simpleMeaning: facts that prove something is true

sentences.daily: There is no evidence of cheating.
sentences.office: There is clear evidence in the audit report.
sentences.interview: I always provide evidence of my achievements.
sentences.business: There is no evidence of market saturation.

words.usageNote: 'Evidence' is usually uncountable.

words.simpleMeaning: new information or a newer version

sentences.daily: There is a new update for this app.
sentences.office: Is there any update on the project?
sentences.interview: I keep my skills updated.
sentences.business: There is a product update coming.
sentences.formal: An update to the policy will be issued.

words.usageNote: 'Update on' (for information) vs 'update of' (for a document).

words.simpleMeaning: written information or the act of observing

sentences.daily: There is a notice on the board.
sentences.office: There is a notice about the holiday.
sentences.interview: I notice small details in every task.
sentences.business: There is a prior notice required.
sentences.formal: The notice must be served in writing.

words.usageNote: 'Notice period' = time before leaving a job.

words.simpleMeaning: a public or official statement

sentences.daily: There was an announcement about the festival.
sentences.office: There is an announcement about the new policy.
sentences.interview: Any announcement about the results?
sentences.business: There will be a product announcement.
sentences.formal: The announcement was made by the CEO.

words.usageNote: 'Make an announcement' is the standard collocation.

words.simpleMeaning: an expression of dissatisfaction

sentences.daily: There has been a complaint about the noise.
sentences.office: There are several complaints from clients.
sentences.interview: How do you handle customer complaints?
sentences.business: There was a complaint about the product.
sentences.informal: I have a complaint about this!

words.usageNote: 'Lodge a complaint' = formally report a complaint.

words.simpleMeaning: a mistake or something wrong

sentences.daily: There is an error in the bill.
sentences.office: There is an error in the code.
sentences.interview: I learn from every error I make.
sentences.business: There is a margin of error in the data.
sentences.formal: The error must be rectified immediately.

words.usageNote: 'Human error' vs 'technical error'. 'Error' is more formal than 'mistake'.

words.simpleMeaning: a problem or topic for discussion

sentences.daily: Is there an issue with the tap?
sentences.office: There is an issue with the network.
sentences.interview: How do you resolve team issues?
sentences.business: There are pricing issues to address.
sentences.formal: The issue must be resolved within 24 hours.

words.usageNote: 'Issue' is milder than 'problem' in formal contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: forward movement or development

sentences.daily: There is good progress in her studies.
sentences.office: Is there any progress on the task?
sentences.interview: I track my progress regularly.
sentences.business: There is steady progress in sales.

words.usageNote: 'Make progress' or 'in progress' are common phrases.

words.simpleMeaning: something that is needed

sentences.daily: There is a requirement for fresh vegetables.
sentences.office: There is a new system requirement.
sentences.interview: What are the key requirements for this role?
sentences.business: There are strict requirements for the tender.

words.usageNote: 'Meet the requirements' = satisfy all conditions.

words.simpleMeaning: being allowed to do something

sentences.daily: Is there permission to park here?
sentences.office: There is a permission process for leave.
sentences.interview: Do I need permission to attend training?
sentences.business: There is a permission required from the board.

words.usageNote: 'With permission' vs 'without permission'.

words.simpleMeaning: the most that is allowed

sentences.daily: There is a speed limit on this road.
sentences.office: There is a budget limit for travel.
sentences.interview: I work within given limits.
sentences.business: There is a production limit per day.
sentences.formal: The limit must not be exceeded.

words.usageNote: 'Exceed the limit' = go beyond what is allowed.

words.simpleMeaning: one of the choices you can make

sentences.daily: There are several options on the menu.
sentences.office: There are two options to resolve this.
sentences.interview: I like to explore all options before deciding.
sentences.business: There are limited options in this market.

words.usageNote: 'Have no option but to' = be forced to do something.

words.simpleMeaning: a strong effect on something

sentences.daily: There is a big impact of social media.
sentences.office: There is a positive impact of this change.
sentences.interview: Can you assess the impact of your decisions?
sentences.business: There is a market impact of this product.
sentences.formal: The impact must be measured carefully.

words.usageNote: 'Impact on' (not 'impact of' when used as a verb).

words.simpleMeaning: a plan or preparation made in advance

sentences.daily: There is an arrangement for travel.
sentences.office: Are there any arrangements for the meeting?
sentences.interview: I make arrangements in advance.
sentences.business: There are arrangements for the product launch.

words.usageNote: 'Make arrangements' is a common phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: the state of something or a requirement

sentences.daily: The road is in bad condition.
sentences.office: There are conditions attached to the offer.
sentences.interview: What are the working conditions?
sentences.business: There are market conditions to consider.
sentences.formal: The condition must be met before signing.

words.usageNote: 'On condition that' = only if. 'Terms and conditions'.

words.simpleMeaning: the chance that something might happen

sentences.daily: There is a possibility of rain.
sentences.office: There is a possibility of promotion.
sentences.interview: Is there a possibility of remote work?
sentences.business: There are many possibilities for growth.

words.usageNote: 'In all possibility' means 'very probably'.

words.simpleMeaning: the circumstances at a particular time

sentences.daily: There is a difficult situation at home.
sentences.office: There was a difficult situation at the office.
sentences.interview: Describe a situation where you led a team.
sentences.business: There is an evolving market situation.

words.usageNote: 'In a situation' vs 'under a situation'. Use 'in'.

words.simpleMeaning: a job, place, or point of view

sentences.daily: There is a good position at the front.
sentences.office: There is an open position in sales.
sentences.interview: What position are you applying for?
sentences.business: There are open positions available.
sentences.formal: The position must be filled immediately.

words.usageNote: 'Position' in jobs context = job role.

words.simpleMeaning: the way something is built or organised

sentences.daily: There is a clear structure to this recipe.
sentences.office: There is a new management structure.
sentences.interview: I follow a structured approach to problem-solving.
sentences.business: There is a corporate structure in place.

words.usageNote: 'Well-structured' = clearly organised.

words.simpleMeaning: an established method of doing something

sentences.daily: There is a procedure for filing documents.
sentences.office: There is a standard procedure to follow.
sentences.interview: I follow all set procedures.
sentences.business: There is a procurement procedure.
sentences.formal: The procedure must be adhered to strictly.

words.usageNote: 'Follow the procedure' is the standard collocation.

words.simpleMeaning: a set of rules or guidelines

sentences.daily: There is a no-smoking policy here.
sentences.office: There is a leave policy in the company.
sentences.interview: What is the company's HR policy?
sentences.business: There is a pricing policy to follow.

words.usageNote: 'Under this policy' = according to this policy.

words.simpleMeaning: an official rule or law

sentences.daily: There is a regulation about noise levels.
sentences.office: There are strict data regulations.
sentences.interview: Are you familiar with industry regulations?
sentences.business: There are export regulations to follow.
sentences.formal: The regulation must be complied with.

words.usageNote: 'Comply with regulations' = follow official rules.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan for how money will be spent

sentences.daily: Is there a budget for the party?
sentences.office: There is a limited budget this quarter.
sentences.interview: How do you manage a tight budget?
sentences.business: There is no budget for new marketing.
sentences.formal: The budget must be approved by the board.

words.usageNote: 'On a budget' = spending carefully.

words.simpleMeaning: the latest time by which something must be done

sentences.daily: Is there a deadline for this form?
sentences.office: There is a tight deadline for this report.
sentences.interview: How do you manage multiple deadlines?
sentences.business: There is a project deadline next Friday.
sentences.formal: The deadline must be strictly observed.

words.usageNote: 'Meet the deadline' = finish on time.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan that gives times for activities

sentences.daily: Is there a schedule for the day?
sentences.office: There is a meeting scheduled for 3 pm.
sentences.interview: Can you follow a strict schedule?
sentences.business: There is a launch schedule prepared.
sentences.formal: The schedule must be adhered to.

words.usageNote: 'On schedule' = on time. 'Behind schedule' = late.

words.simpleMeaning: a list of items to be discussed

sentences.daily: Is there an agenda for today's meeting?
sentences.office: There are five items on the agenda.
sentences.interview: How do you set your daily agenda?
sentences.business: There is a clear agenda for the summit.
sentences.formal: The agenda was circulated in advance.

words.usageNote: 'Hidden agenda' = secret motive.

words.simpleMeaning: a goal that you try to achieve

sentences.daily: Is there a target for this month?
sentences.office: There is a sales target for Q3.
sentences.interview: How do you meet your targets?
sentences.business: There is a revenue target to achieve.
sentences.formal: The target must be met by year-end.

words.usageNote: 'On target' = progressing as planned.

words.simpleMeaning: a decision made between two or more parties

sentences.daily: Is there an agreement on the meeting time?
sentences.office: There is a service agreement in place.
sentences.interview: Have you worked under formal agreements?
sentences.business: There is a non-disclosure agreement.
sentences.formal: The agreement must be signed by both parties.

words.usageNote: 'Come to an agreement' or 'reach an agreement'.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan or suggestion put forward for consideration

sentences.daily: Is there a proposal for the weekend trip?
sentences.office: There is a new proposal on the table.
sentences.interview: How do you prepare a project proposal?
sentences.business: There is a business proposal under review.
sentences.formal: The proposal must be submitted by Friday.

words.usageNote: 'Put forward a proposal' or 'submit a proposal'.

words.simpleMeaning: an approximate calculation

sentences.daily: Is there an estimate for the repair?
sentences.office: There is a cost estimate for the project.
sentences.interview: How do you estimate project timelines?
sentences.business: There is a rough estimate for the budget.

words.usageNote: 'Rough estimate' = approximate figure.

words.simpleMeaning: a meeting arranged for a specific time

sentences.daily: Is there an appointment with the doctor?
sentences.office: There is a client appointment at 4 pm.
sentences.interview: I arrived early for the appointment.
sentences.business: There is a board appointment ceremony.
sentences.formal: The appointment must be confirmed in writing.

words.usageNote: 'Keep an appointment' = attend it; 'cancel an appointment'.

words.simpleMeaning: information about how to do something

sentences.daily: Are there any instructions?
sentences.office: There are clear instructions in the manual.
sentences.interview: I follow instructions carefully.
sentences.business: There are instructions for using the product.
sentences.formal: The instruction must be followed precisely.

words.usageNote: 'Follow instructions' or 'carry out instructions'.

words.simpleMeaning: comments about how well someone did something

sentences.daily: Is there any feedback on my work?
sentences.office: There is positive feedback from the client.
sentences.interview: I welcome constructive feedback.
sentences.business: There is customer feedback to analyse.
sentences.formal: Feedback must be submitted by Monday.

words.usageNote: 'Feedback' is uncountable in standard usage.

words.simpleMeaning: a serious disagreement or fight

sentences.daily: There is a conflict between the two neighbours.
sentences.office: There is a conflict of interest here.
sentences.interview: How do you resolve team conflict?
sentences.business: There is a conflict in the negotiation.
sentences.formal: The conflict must be resolved amicably.

words.usageNote: 'Conflict of interest' = situation creating bias.

words.simpleMeaning: a serious and dangerous situation

sentences.daily: Is there an emergency exit here?
sentences.office: There was an emergency drill today.
sentences.interview: How do you respond in an emergency?
sentences.business: There is an emergency meeting called.
sentences.formal: The emergency must be contained immediately.

words.usageNote: 'Emergency exit' / 'emergency service' are common compounds.

words.simpleMeaning: the most important thing to do first

sentences.daily: There is a priority list to follow.
sentences.office: Safety is a top priority.
sentences.interview: How do you set your priorities?
sentences.business: There is a business priority for Q1.
sentences.formal: The priority must be established clearly.

words.usageNote: 'Top priority' = most important. 'Low priority' = less urgent.

words.simpleMeaning: something done successfully

sentences.daily: There is a big achievement to celebrate.
sentences.office: There are many achievements this year.
sentences.interview: What is your biggest achievement?
sentences.business: There are several business achievements.

words.usageNote: 'Academic achievement', 'professional achievement' are common phrases.

words.simpleMeaning: something given to help

sentences.daily: There is a contribution from each member.
sentences.office: Your contribution to the project is valued.
sentences.interview: What contribution can you make to our team?
sentences.business: There are many contributions from stakeholders.

words.usageNote: 'Make a contribution to' is the standard collocation.

words.simpleMeaning: a duty you are required to do

sentences.daily: There is a responsibility to clean.
sentences.office: There are responsibilities in this role.
sentences.interview: Can you handle multiple responsibilities?
sentences.business: There are financial responsibilities.

words.usageNote: 'Take responsibility' = accept that you caused something.

words.simpleMeaning: a belief that something will happen

sentences.daily: Is there an expectation about the result?
sentences.office: There are high expectations from this team.
sentences.interview: What are your salary expectations?
sentences.business: There are client expectations to meet.
sentences.formal: The expectation must be communicated clearly.

words.usageNote: 'Meet expectations' = do what was expected.

words.simpleMeaning: a result of an action

sentences.daily: There are consequences for being late.
sentences.office: There are serious consequences for data breaches.
sentences.interview: Think about the consequences of each decision.
sentences.business: There are market consequences of this policy.

words.usageNote: 'As a consequence' = therefore, as a result.

words.simpleMeaning: a different choice available

sentences.daily: Is there an alternative route?
sentences.office: There are no alternatives to this software.
sentences.interview: Do you have an alternative approach?
sentences.business: There are alternative suppliers.

words.usageNote: 'Have no alternative but to' = be forced to.

words.simpleMeaning: a case where the rule does not apply

sentences.daily: There is an exception for children.
sentences.office: Are there any exceptions to this policy?
sentences.interview: I treat every case without exception.
sentences.business: There are no exceptions in compliance.
sentences.informal: Is there an exception for me?

words.usageNote: 'Without exception' = in every case, no exceptions.

words.simpleMeaning: a suggestion that something is good

sentences.daily: Is there a recommendation for a good doctor?
sentences.office: There are recommendations from the audit.
sentences.interview: Do you have a recommendation letter?
sentences.business: There are client recommendations.
sentences.formal: The recommendation must be acted on.

words.usageNote: 'Letter of recommendation' = formal support letter.

words.simpleMeaning: watching and directing activities

sentences.daily: There is supervision on the playground.
sentences.office: There is a supervision rota.
sentences.interview: Do you work well under supervision?
sentences.business: There is no direct supervision needed.

words.usageNote: 'Under supervision' = with someone watching.

words.simpleMeaning: an official check of something

sentences.daily: There is an inspection of the building.
sentences.office: There was a surprise inspection today.
sentences.interview: Have you conducted inspections before?
sentences.business: There are quarterly inspections.
sentences.formal: The inspection was carried out as per procedure.

words.usageNote: 'Carry out an inspection' or 'conduct an inspection'.

words.simpleMeaning: keeping something in good condition

sentences.daily: There is a maintenance schedule for the car.
sentences.office: There is a system maintenance window tonight.
sentences.interview: Tell me about a maintenance procedure you followed.
sentences.business: There is a maintenance contract in place.
sentences.formal: Maintenance must be carried out monthly.

words.usageNote: 'Preventive maintenance' = to stop problems before they occur.

words.simpleMeaning: something that takes the place of another

sentences.daily: Is there a replacement for this part?
sentences.office: Is there a replacement for the absent employee?
sentences.interview: Are you open to a replacement role?
sentences.business: There is no replacement for customer trust.

words.usageNote: 'Replacement for' (not 'replacement of').

words.simpleMeaning: presenting something or someone for the first time

sentences.daily: There was a brief introduction.
sentences.office: There was an introduction to the new software.
sentences.interview: Give a brief introduction of yourself.
sentences.business: There is an introduction section in the report.

words.usageNote: 'Brief introduction' = short presentation of oneself or a topic.

words.simpleMeaning: the process of growth or improvement

sentences.daily: There is a lot of development in this area.
sentences.office: There is a new software development project.
sentences.interview: Tell me about your professional development.
sentences.business: There is rapid business development.

words.usageNote: 'Research and development (R&D)' is a key business concept.

words.simpleMeaning: the process of judging or assessing

sentences.daily: Is there an evaluation today?
sentences.office: There is a performance evaluation this week.
sentences.interview: Describe your self-evaluation process.
sentences.business: There is a vendor evaluation underway.
sentences.formal: A formal evaluation must be conducted.

words.usageNote: 'Under evaluation' = being assessed currently.

words.simpleMeaning: handing over a document or application

sentences.daily: Is there a submission for the competition?
sentences.office: There is a report submission due tomorrow.
sentences.interview: I made a presentation submission.
sentences.business: There are tender submissions pending.
sentences.formal: The submission must meet the deadline.

words.usageNote: 'Make a submission' = to formally submit something.

words.simpleMeaning: a message telling you about something

sentences.daily: There is a notification on my phone.
sentences.office: There is a notification about system downtime.
sentences.interview: I have a notification from the company.
sentences.business: There is a customer notification process.
sentences.formal: Notification must be given 7 days in advance.

words.usageNote: 'Push notification' = alerts from apps.

words.simpleMeaning: a statement that something is definitely true

sentences.daily: Is there a confirmation of the booking?
sentences.office: There is no confirmation from the client.
sentences.interview: I sent a confirmation email.
sentences.business: There is a payment confirmation.

words.usageNote: 'Awaiting confirmation' = waiting for approval.

words.simpleMeaning: a situation where people try to win or be best

sentences.daily: There is a lot of competition in the market.
sentences.office: There is competition for the promotion.
sentences.interview: How do you handle competition?
sentences.business: There is tough competition from global brands.

words.usageNote: 'Healthy competition' = beneficial rivalry.

words.simpleMeaning: an event to mark something special

sentences.daily: There will be a celebration tonight.
sentences.office: There is a success celebration tomorrow.
sentences.interview: How do you celebrate team success?
sentences.business: There is a product launch celebration.
sentences.formal: The celebration was held at a five-star venue.

words.usageNote: 'In celebration of' = in honour of something.

words.simpleMeaning: praise for a good achievement

sentences.daily: There is recognition for her hard work.
sentences.office: There is an employee recognition programme.
sentences.interview: How important is recognition to you?
sentences.business: There is brand recognition globally.
sentences.formal: The recognition must be formally conveyed.

words.usageNote: 'Brand recognition' = how well a brand is known.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to change or adapt easily

sentences.daily: There is flexibility in the schedule.
sentences.office: Is there flexibility in working hours?
sentences.interview: I value flexibility in the workplace.
sentences.business: There is little flexibility in the contract.

words.usageNote: 'Work flexibility' = flexible working arrangements.

words.simpleMeaning: being open and honest about actions

sentences.daily: There is transparency in their dealings.
sentences.office: There is a culture of transparency here.
sentences.interview: How do you ensure transparency in your work?
sentences.business: There is a lack of transparency in the accounts.
sentences.formal: Transparency must be maintained at all levels.

words.usageNote: 'Corporate transparency' = companies being open about practices.

words.simpleMeaning: being responsible for your actions

sentences.daily: There is a need for accountability.
sentences.office: There is accountability at every level.
sentences.interview: How do you demonstrate accountability?
sentences.business: There is financial accountability to investors.
sentences.formal: Accountability must be embedded in the process.

words.usageNote: 'Hold accountable' = make someone answer for their actions.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to start things independently

sentences.daily: There is a community initiative here.
sentences.office: Is there a new company initiative?
sentences.interview: Tell me about an initiative you led.
sentences.business: There is a CSR initiative underway.
sentences.formal: The initiative must be formally launched.

words.usageNote: 'Take initiative' = act without being told.

words.simpleMeaning: working together to achieve a goal

sentences.daily: There is great collaboration in this project.
sentences.office: There is a collaboration between two teams.
sentences.interview: Describe a successful collaboration.
sentences.business: There is a strategic collaboration.

words.usageNote: 'In collaboration with' = working with someone.

words.simpleMeaning: the introduction of new ideas or methods

sentences.daily: There is so much innovation today.
sentences.office: There is a culture of innovation here.
sentences.interview: How do you bring innovation to your work?
sentences.business: There is room for innovation.
sentences.formal: Innovation must be encouraged at all levels.

words.usageNote: 'Disruptive innovation' = innovation that changes an industry.

words.simpleMeaning: achieving maximum results with minimum waste

sentences.daily: There is high efficiency in this process.
sentences.office: There is room for more efficiency.
sentences.interview: How do you improve efficiency?
sentences.business: There is an efficiency-first approach.

words.usageNote: 'Energy efficiency' = using less energy for the same output.

words.simpleMeaning: keeping information private

sentences.daily: There is confidentiality in this matter.
sentences.office: There is a strict confidentiality clause.
sentences.interview: How do you maintain confidentiality?
sentences.business: There is confidentiality in client data.

words.usageNote: 'Breach of confidentiality' = wrongly revealing private information.

words.simpleMeaning: behaving in a skilled and competent way

sentences.daily: There is a sense of professionalism here.
sentences.office: There is high professionalism in this team.
sentences.interview: How would you define professionalism?
sentences.business: There is a need for professionalism.
sentences.formal: Professionalism must be demonstrated at all times.

words.usageNote: 'Lack of professionalism' = behaving unprofessionally.

words.simpleMeaning: the quality of being trusted

sentences.daily: There is credibility in his words.
sentences.office: There is a question about this data's credibility.
sentences.interview: How do you build your credibility?
sentences.business: There is credibility in our brand.

words.usageNote: 'Credibility gap' = difference between what is said and done.

words.simpleMeaning: the length of time something lasts

sentences.daily: What is the duration of the course?
sentences.office: There is a fixed duration for the contract.
sentences.interview: What was the duration of your last project?
sentences.business: There is a project duration of six months.
sentences.formal: The duration must be specified in the agreement.

words.usageNote: 'For the duration of' = for the whole time.

words.simpleMeaning: how often something happens

sentences.daily: There is a high frequency of buses here.
sentences.office: There is a high frequency of client calls.
sentences.interview: What frequency do you report to your manager?
sentences.business: There is a high frequency of transactions.

words.usageNote: 'High frequency' vs 'low frequency'.

words.simpleMeaning: variety of different types or people

sentences.daily: There is diversity in this community.
sentences.office: There is great diversity in the team.
sentences.interview: How do you contribute to diversity?
sentences.business: There is diversity in the product range.

words.usageNote: 'Diversity and inclusion' is a key corporate concept.

words.simpleMeaning: the reason why someone does something

sentences.daily: There is a lot of motivation here.
sentences.office: There is a team motivation programme.
sentences.interview: What is your motivation for this job?
sentences.business: There is motivation behind every decision.

words.usageNote: 'Self-motivation' = driving yourself without external push.

words.simpleMeaning: the quality of being honest and having strong morals

sentences.daily: There is integrity in his actions.
sentences.office: There is a culture of integrity here.
sentences.interview: How do you maintain integrity at work?
sentences.business: There is integrity in our business dealings.

words.usageNote: 'Personal integrity' vs 'data integrity'.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan to achieve a goal

sentences.daily: Is there a strategy for the trip?
sentences.office: There is a new marketing strategy.
sentences.interview: What is your strategy for this role?
sentences.business: There is a long-term business strategy.

words.usageNote: 'Strategic plan' = long-term plan with goals.

words.simpleMeaning: a general rule or piece of advice

sentences.daily: There are guidelines for the trip.
sentences.office: There are strict guidelines to follow.
sentences.interview: How do you use guidelines in your work?
sentences.business: There are industry guidelines.

words.usageNote: 'Follow the guidelines' = act according to instructions.

words.simpleMeaning: the process of putting a plan into action

sentences.daily: Is there an implementation plan?
sentences.office: There is an implementation phase for the project.
sentences.interview: Describe your implementation experience.
sentences.business: There is a phased implementation.

words.usageNote: 'Implementation plan' vs 'execution plan'.

words.simpleMeaning: organising people/activities effectively

sentences.daily: There is great coordination in the team.
sentences.office: There is coordination between departments.
sentences.interview: How do you ensure coordination?
sentences.business: There is inter-departmental coordination.

words.usageNote: 'Lack of coordination' = poor alignment between teams.

words.simpleMeaning: the exchange of information

sentences.daily: There is good communication here.
sentences.office: There is a communication gap in the team.
sentences.interview: Strong communication is my key strength.
sentences.business: There is a communication strategy in place.

words.usageNote: 'Communication skills' = ability to convey information clearly.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal discussion to reach an agreement

sentences.daily: Is there a negotiation on the price?
sentences.office: There is a salary negotiation.
sentences.interview: How do you handle negotiations?
sentences.business: There is a contract negotiation.
sentences.formal: The negotiation must be conducted formally.

words.usageNote: 'Win-win negotiation' = both parties benefit.

words.simpleMeaning: the distribution of resources for a purpose

sentences.daily: Is there a budget allocation for this?
sentences.office: There is a resource allocation meeting.
sentences.interview: Have you managed budget allocations?
sentences.business: There is an allocation for marketing.

words.usageNote: 'Budget allocation' = money set aside for a purpose.

words.simpleMeaning: a forecast or estimate about the future

sentences.daily: Are there projections for next year?
sentences.office: There are revenue projections for Q4.
sentences.interview: How do you make financial projections?
sentences.business: There are sales projections to meet.

words.usageNote: 'Sales projection' = predicted future sales.

words.simpleMeaning: keeping something or someone in place

sentences.daily: Is there a retention bonus?
sentences.office: There is a low employee retention rate.
sentences.interview: What strategies do you use for retention?
sentences.business: There is a customer retention focus.

words.usageNote: 'Customer retention' vs 'employee retention'.

words.simpleMeaning: how well someone or something does a task

sentences.daily: There is a high-performance culture.
sentences.office: There is a performance review this week.
sentences.interview: Tell me about your best performance.
sentences.business: There is a strong sales performance.

words.usageNote: 'Performance appraisal' = formal assessment of work.

words.simpleMeaning: total sales or rate of employee change

sentences.daily: Is there a high staff turnover here?
sentences.office: There is a 20% annual turnover.
sentences.interview: What was the company's turnover?
sentences.business: There is a growing annual turnover.

words.usageNote: 'Staff turnover' = rate at which employees leave.

words.simpleMeaning: repayment of money spent

sentences.daily: Is there a reimbursement for travel?
sentences.office: There is a travel reimbursement policy.
sentences.interview: Are there reimbursements for training?
sentences.business: There is a client reimbursement clause.
sentences.formal: Reimbursement must be claimed within 30 days.

words.usageNote: 'Claim reimbursement' = ask to be paid back.

words.simpleMeaning: following rules and regulations

sentences.daily: Is there a compliance requirement?
sentences.office: There is a compliance audit next week.
sentences.interview: Are you familiar with compliance norms?
sentences.business: There is strict regulatory compliance.

words.usageNote: 'In compliance with' = following the rules.

words.simpleMeaning: a standard used for comparison

sentences.daily: Is there a benchmark for this task?
sentences.office: There is a performance benchmark.
sentences.interview: How do you set benchmarks?
sentences.business: There is a market benchmark to follow.

words.usageNote: 'Industry benchmark' = a standard measure in a sector.

words.simpleMeaning: something that encourages action

sentences.daily: Is there an incentive for completing this?
sentences.office: There are performance incentives.
sentences.interview: What incentives motivate you?
sentences.business: There are sales incentives.
sentences.formal: An incentive scheme must be designed.

words.usageNote: 'Performance incentive' = reward for meeting targets.

words.simpleMeaning: a guarantee that a product will work

sentences.daily: Is there a warranty on this phone?
sentences.office: There is a warranty policy.
sentences.interview: Explain the warranty terms to a customer.
sentences.business: There is a one-year warranty.

words.usageNote: 'Under warranty' = still covered by the guarantee.

words.simpleMeaning: legal responsibility for something

sentences.daily: Is there a liability for this?
sentences.office: There is a legal liability here.
sentences.interview: Are you aware of your liabilities?
sentences.business: There are financial liabilities.

words.usageNote: 'Limited liability' = maximum loss is capped.

words.simpleMeaning: proving that something is true or correct

sentences.daily: Is there a validation for this form?
sentences.office: There is a data validation process.
sentences.interview: How do you ensure validation?
sentences.business: There is a product validation stage.

words.usageNote: 'Data validation' = checking data is correct.

words.simpleMeaning: making something as effective as possible

sentences.daily: Is there any optimisation possible here?
sentences.office: There is an SEO optimisation project.
sentences.interview: How do you approach process optimisation?
sentences.business: There is a cost optimisation strategy.

words.usageNote: 'Search engine optimisation (SEO)' = improving website ranking.

words.simpleMeaning: making information publicly known

sentences.daily: Is there any disclosure required?
sentences.office: There is a disclosure policy.
sentences.interview: Are there any financial disclosures?
sentences.business: There is a full disclosure clause.
sentences.formal: Disclosure must be made to regulators.

words.usageNote: 'Non-disclosure agreement (NDA)' = a contract to keep information secret.

words.simpleMeaning: resolving disputes through a third party

sentences.daily: Is there arbitration for this dispute?
sentences.office: There is an arbitration clause.
sentences.interview: Have you handled arbitration cases?
sentences.business: There is mandatory arbitration.

words.usageNote: 'Binding arbitration' = the decision must be followed.

words.simpleMeaning: maintaining resources for future use

sentences.daily: There is a focus on sustainability.
sentences.office: There is a sustainability report.
sentences.interview: How do you incorporate sustainability?
sentences.business: There is a sustainability initiative.
sentences.formal: Sustainability must be embedded in policy.

words.usageNote: 'Corporate sustainability' = long-term responsible business.

words.simpleMeaning: guidance from an experienced person

sentences.daily: Is there a mentorship programme?
sentences.office: There is a mentorship culture here.
sentences.interview: Have you had a mentor or been a mentor?
sentences.business: There is a mentorship scheme for juniors.

words.usageNote: 'Mentor' is the person; 'mentee' is the one being helped.

words.simpleMeaning: a period of work experience for students

sentences.daily: Is there an internship available?
sentences.office: There is an internship programme.
sentences.interview: Tell me about your internship experience.
sentences.business: There is an annual internship intake.

words.usageNote: 'Paid internship' vs 'unpaid internship'.

words.simpleMeaning: building professional contacts

sentences.daily: There is a networking event today.
sentences.office: There is a networking opportunity.
sentences.interview: How important is networking to you?
sentences.business: There is a strong networking culture.

words.usageNote: 'Professional networking' = building career contacts.

words.simpleMeaning: a licence to sell another's goods or services

sentences.daily: Is there a franchise here?
sentences.office: There is a franchise model.
sentences.interview: Have you worked in a franchise?
sentences.business: There is a franchise opportunity.
sentences.formal: A franchise agreement must be signed.

words.usageNote: 'Franchisee' = person who buys the franchise.

words.simpleMeaning: the promotion of a company's image

sentences.daily: Is there branding on the product?
sentences.office: There is a rebranding project.
sentences.interview: What is your approach to branding?
sentences.business: There is a branding strategy.

words.usageNote: 'Personal branding' = how you present yourself professionally.

words.simpleMeaning: efforts to communicate with a wider audience

sentences.daily: Is there a community outreach programme?
sentences.office: There is a customer outreach strategy.
sentences.interview: Describe your outreach experience.
sentences.business: There is a sales outreach campaign.

words.usageNote: 'Outreach programme' = a plan to engage new people.

words.simpleMeaning: time when a machine or system is not working

sentences.daily: There is some downtime for maintenance.
sentences.office: There is planned system downtime.
sentences.interview: How do you handle server downtime?
sentences.business: There is minimal downtime here.
sentences.formal: Downtime must be communicated in advance.

words.usageNote: 'Planned downtime' vs 'unplanned downtime'.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to grow or expand easily

sentences.daily: Is there scalability in this plan?
sentences.office: There is a scalability challenge.
sentences.interview: How do you ensure scalability?
sentences.business: There is good scalability in this model.

words.usageNote: 'Scalable solution' = one that works as it grows.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to be seen or noticed

sentences.daily: There is good visibility today.
sentences.office: There is a need for better brand visibility.
sentences.interview: How do you increase your professional visibility?
sentences.business: There is increased market visibility.

words.usageNote: 'Brand visibility' = how well a brand is seen in the market.

words.simpleMeaning: the sequence of steps in a process

sentences.daily: Is there a workflow for this?
sentences.office: There is an efficient workflow in place.
sentences.interview: Describe your typical workflow.
sentences.business: There is a streamlined workflow.

words.usageNote: 'Workflow automation' = automating repetitive tasks.

words.simpleMeaning: a person with an interest in a project

sentences.daily: Are there stakeholders in this project?
sentences.office: There are key stakeholders to update.
sentences.interview: How do you manage stakeholders?
sentences.business: There are multiple stakeholders.

words.usageNote: 'Stakeholder management' = keeping all relevant people informed.

words.simpleMeaning: an important stage in a process

sentences.daily: Is there a milestone to celebrate?
sentences.office: There are project milestones.
sentences.interview: What milestones have you achieved?
sentences.business: There are quarterly business milestones.

words.usageNote: 'Reach a milestone' = achieve an important goal.

words.simpleMeaning: income generated by a business

sentences.daily: Is there revenue from this?
sentences.office: There is a revenue target for this quarter.
sentences.interview: How have you contributed to revenue growth?
sentences.business: There is a steady revenue stream.

words.usageNote: 'Revenue stream' = a source of ongoing income.

words.simpleMeaning: money spent on something

sentences.daily: Is there an expenditure limit?
sentences.office: There is a capital expenditure plan.
sentences.interview: How do you manage expenditure?
sentences.business: There is high operating expenditure.

words.usageNote: 'Capital expenditure (CapEx)' vs 'operating expenditure (OpEx)'.

words.simpleMeaning: the efficiency of output

sentences.daily: There is high productivity here.
sentences.office: There is a productivity improvement drive.
sentences.interview: How do you boost productivity?
sentences.business: There is a productivity benchmark.

words.usageNote: 'Boost productivity' = increase output.

words.simpleMeaning: job loss or unnecessary duplication

sentences.daily: Is there a risk of redundancy?
sentences.office: There have been redundancies in the company.
sentences.interview: Have you experienced redundancy?
sentences.business: There is a redundancy payment.
sentences.formal: Redundancy must be handled with care.

words.usageNote: 'Made redundant' = lost job due to the role no longer being needed.

words.simpleMeaning: introduction to a new job or place

sentences.daily: Is there an orientation for new students?
sentences.office: There is an employee orientation programme.
sentences.interview: What is the orientation process?
sentences.business: There is a client orientation session.
sentences.formal: Orientation must be completed on day one.

words.usageNote: 'New employee orientation' = induction for new joiners.

words.simpleMeaning: formal evaluation of performance

sentences.daily: Is there an appraisal this month?
sentences.office: There is an annual performance appraisal.
sentences.interview: How do you prepare for appraisals?
sentences.business: There are quarterly appraisals.

words.usageNote: '360-degree appraisal' = feedback from all directions.

words.simpleMeaning: gradual reduction of staff through departures

sentences.daily: Is there high attrition here?
sentences.office: There is a high attrition rate.
sentences.interview: How have you managed attrition?
sentences.business: There is an attrition concern.

words.usageNote: 'Natural attrition' = staff leaving of their own accord.

words.simpleMeaning: the process of buying goods or services

sentences.daily: Is there a procurement process?
sentences.office: There is a procurement team.
sentences.interview: What is your procurement experience?
sentences.business: There is a procurement strategy.

words.usageNote: 'Procurement cycle' = end-to-end purchasing process.

words.simpleMeaning: the coordination of complex operations

sentences.daily: Is there a logistics plan?
sentences.office: There are logistics issues to address.
sentences.interview: What is your logistics experience?
sentences.business: There is a strong logistics network.

words.usageNote: 'Third-party logistics (3PL)' = outsourcing supply chain.

words.simpleMeaning: taking legal action through court

sentences.daily: Is there a litigation risk?
sentences.office: There is pending litigation.
sentences.interview: Have you dealt with litigation?
sentences.business: There is a litigation strategy.
sentences.formal: Litigation must be avoided where possible.

words.usageNote: 'Avoid litigation' = prefer settlement over court.

words.simpleMeaning: using something to its maximum advantage

sentences.daily: Is there a way to leverage this?
sentences.office: There is an opportunity to leverage technology.
sentences.interview: How do you leverage your skills?
sentences.business: There is a leverage in this deal.

words.usageNote: 'Leverage (noun)' = power to influence; 'leverage (verb)' = use to your advantage.

words.simpleMeaning: experience of or being at risk from something

sentences.daily: There is good exposure in this role.
sentences.office: There is international exposure here.
sentences.interview: What kind of exposure do you have?
sentences.business: There is limited market exposure.
sentences.formal: Exposure to risk must be migratable.

words.usageNote: 'Exposure to' = having experience of or contact with.

words.simpleMeaning: changing from one form or state to another

sentences.daily: Is there a conversion rate?
sentences.office: There is a lead conversion issue.
sentences.interview: How do you improve conversion rates?
sentences.business: There is a conversion funnel.

words.usageNote: 'Conversion rate' = percentage of leads that become customers.

words.simpleMeaning: an official inspection of accounts

sentences.daily: Is there an audit today?
sentences.office: There is a surprise audit tomorrow.
sentences.interview: Have you been through an audit?
sentences.business: There is an annual audit.
sentences.formal: The audit must be conducted independently.

words.usageNote: 'Internal audit' vs 'external audit'.

words.simpleMeaning: an amount by which something falls short

sentences.daily: Is there a deficit in the budget?
sentences.office: There is a budget deficit this quarter.
sentences.interview: How have you managed a deficit situation?
sentences.business: There is a trade deficit.

words.usageNote: 'Budget deficit' = spending more than income.

words.simpleMeaning: ongoing business costs not tied to production

sentences.daily: Is there high overhead here?
sentences.office: There are high office overheads.
sentences.interview: How do you manage overhead costs?
sentences.business: There is a need to reduce overheads.

words.usageNote: 'Reduce overheads' = cut fixed operating costs.

words.simpleMeaning: a change made to improve something

sentences.daily: Is there an amendment to the plan?
sentences.office: There is an amendment to the contract.
sentences.interview: Have you drafted amendments?
sentences.business: There are proposed amendments.

words.usageNote: 'Move an amendment' = propose a change formally.

words.simpleMeaning: a specific section of a legal document

sentences.daily: Is there a clause about this?
sentences.office: There is a confidentiality clause.
sentences.interview: Have you reviewed contract clauses?
sentences.business: There is a penalty clause.

words.usageNote: 'Penalty clause' = clause that imposes a fine for non-compliance.

words.simpleMeaning: the level at which something starts to happen

sentences.daily: Is there a pain threshold?
sentences.office: There is a threshold for approval.
sentences.interview: What is your personal threshold for stress?
sentences.business: There is a minimum order threshold.
sentences.formal: The threshold must not be exceeded.

words.usageNote: 'Below threshold' = not yet at the required level.

words.simpleMeaning: a group of organisations working together

sentences.daily: Is there a consortium involved?
sentences.office: There is a consortium bidding on this project.
sentences.interview: Have you worked within a consortium?
sentences.business: There is a procurement consortium.

words.usageNote: 'Bidding consortium' = group of companies submitting a joint bid.

words.simpleMeaning: the force that keeps something going

sentences.daily: Is there momentum in this project?
sentences.office: There is great momentum in the team.
sentences.interview: How do you build momentum?
sentences.business: There is market momentum here.

words.usageNote: 'Build momentum' = get stronger as you go.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan for an unexpected event

sentences.daily: Is there a contingency plan?
sentences.office: There is a contingency budget.
sentences.interview: How do you plan for contingencies?
sentences.business: There is a contingency reserve.
sentences.formal: A contingency plan must be in place.

words.usageNote: 'Contingency plan' = a backup strategy for unexpected events.

words.simpleMeaning: a high level of skill

sentences.daily: Is there a proficiency test?
sentences.office: There is a language proficiency requirement.
sentences.interview: What is your proficiency in this software?
sentences.business: There is a proficiency benchmark.

words.usageNote: 'Language proficiency' = ability in a specific language.

words.simpleMeaning: the basic physical systems of a country or organisation

sentences.daily: Is there good infrastructure here?
sentences.office: There is a lack of IT infrastructure.
sentences.interview: What is your experience in infrastructure?
sentences.business: There is major infrastructure investment.

words.usageNote: 'IT infrastructure' = hardware, software, and networks.

words.simpleMeaning: money given by the government to lower prices

sentences.daily: Is there a subsidy for this?
sentences.office: There is a government subsidy scheme.
sentences.interview: Have you worked with subsidies?
sentences.business: There is a farming subsidy.
sentences.formal: The subsidy must be claimed properly.

words.usageNote: 'Farm subsidy' = government payment to farmers.

words.simpleMeaning: a tax on imports or exports

sentences.daily: Is there a tariff on this product?
sentences.office: There is a new import tariff.
sentences.interview: Are you familiar with tariff structures?
sentences.business: There are rising tariffs on steel.

words.usageNote: 'Import tariff' = tax on goods brought into a country.

words.simpleMeaning: a symbol or name registered to represent a brand

sentences.daily: Is there a trademark on this product?
sentences.office: There is a trademark registration.
sentences.interview: Are you familiar with trademark law?
sentences.business: There is a trademark dispute.

words.usageNote: 'Registered trademark (™ or ®)' = legally protected brand symbol.

words.simpleMeaning: legal right to control use of original work

sentences.daily: Is there a copyright on this music?
sentences.office: There is a copyright notice.
sentences.interview: How do you handle copyright issues?
sentences.business: There is a copyright infringement claim.

words.usageNote: 'Copyright infringement' = using someone's work without permission.

words.simpleMeaning: a first model of something new

sentences.daily: Is there a prototype to test?
sentences.office: There is a prototype ready for review.
sentences.interview: Have you built a prototype?
sentences.business: There is a product prototype.

words.usageNote: 'MVP (minimum viable product)' is a type of prototype.

words.simpleMeaning: a person or company that sells goods

sentences.daily: Is there a good vendor nearby?
sentences.office: There is a vendor meeting today.
sentences.interview: How do you manage vendors?
sentences.business: There is a preferred vendor list.

words.usageNote: 'Vendor management' = process of overseeing suppliers.

words.simpleMeaning: a legal agreement between parties

sentences.daily: Is there a contract for this?
sentences.office: There is a contract to be signed.
sentences.interview: Have you worked on contract terms?
sentences.business: There is a long-term contract.
sentences.formal: The contract must be reviewed by legal.

words.usageNote: 'Breach of contract' = failing to follow a contract.

words.simpleMeaning: a complete list of goods or materials

sentences.daily: Is there an inventory check today?
sentences.office: There is an inventory management system.
sentences.interview: How do you manage inventory?
sentences.business: There is excess inventory.

words.usageNote: 'Inventory control' = managing stock levels.

words.simpleMeaning: a payment from a company's profit to shareholders

sentences.daily: Is there a dividend this year?
sentences.office: There is a quarterly dividend payment.
sentences.interview: How are dividends calculated?
sentences.business: There is a dividend policy.

words.usageNote: 'Pay a dividend' = distribute profit to shareholders.

words.simpleMeaning: restoring good relations or matching accounts

sentences.daily: Is there a reconciliation in the accounts?
sentences.office: There is a monthly bank reconciliation.
sentences.interview: Have you done account reconciliation?
sentences.business: There is a data reconciliation issue.
sentences.formal: Reconciliation must be completed by month-end.

words.usageNote: 'Bank reconciliation' = matching company records to bank statement.

words.simpleMeaning: how practical a plan is

sentences.daily: Is there feasibility in this plan?
sentences.office: There is a feasibility study underway.
sentences.interview: How do you assess feasibility?
sentences.business: There is no feasibility in this idea.

words.usageNote: 'Feasibility study' = research to check if a plan is possible.
